pattern: With at most 100 elements and values at most 100, the described process is cheap enough to simply simulate directly for every zero starting position and both directions, counting how many runs finish with every element at zero.
hints: The process is fully deterministic once you fix a starting index and direction, so just simulate it exactly as described.

At each step: if you're out of bounds, stop; if the current cell is 0, move one step in the current direction; otherwise, decrement that cell, flip your direction, and take one step in the new direction.
A starting choice is valid only if, after the simulation ends, every element of the array is exactly 0; count how many of the (zero position, direction) pairs satisfy this.

edge_inputs.description: a zero flanked by large values that never fully cancel out
multiple zero starting points but only some directions succeed

stress.description: random small arrays guaranteed to contain at least one zero
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 30)))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    zero_at = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    nums[zero_at] = 0
    return [nums]

complexity.time: O(n * maxVal) simulated per candidate start
